decisions.summary: SCOPE SPLIT (operator-confirmed): this issue ships the Sequencer role, Backlog page, sequence.md source-of-truth, SQLite cache, in-pipeline pinning, the operator Ready/Block pickup gate, condition flags, and the AI-suggested planning-policy FIELD (store + AI-suggest + operator-edit + preserve-across-re-sequence + legacy-label read). The live-Flywheel surgery (consume-sequence as pickup order, stop self-ranking, eligibility filter, emergency-promote, planning-policy launch-routing) = D8/work-item-9 is DEFERRED to a follow-up issue.

The Sequencer is a project-scoped, server-spawned special role mirroring the flywheel orchestrator: spawnRun + fixed agentId 'backlog-sequencer' + singleton gate + allowHost + workspace=process.cwd(). It is NOT a work agent (don't use Claude's Agent tool) and NOT a persistent tick-loop — it is ONE-SHOT per regenerate trigger (spawn -> produce candidate -> finalize -> exit).

Preservation/pinning/gate/edge-provenance are enforced in CODE (reconcileSequence), never trusted to the LLM (consistent with the deterministic-synthesis principle, PAN-1864). The agent writes a candidate ranking (a SequenceDoc JSON); an internal `pan backlog finalize` command runs reconcileSequence(prior, candidate) -> writeSequence (renders md + queueAutoCommit) -> rebuild SQLite cache. This mirrors `pan plan finalize` exactly.

Chunked map-reduce for creation/review (NFR-6) = code-side batch partitioning (planBatches(issues, batchSize)) + a running-shortlist agent loop over per-batch files; the agent never holds all 522 bodies in context. Incremental reads only the prior sequence.md + the changed bodies. The chunking LOGIC is unit-tested (N issues -> ceil(N/K) batches), independent of agent runtime behavior.

Shared BacklogSequence node/edge/enum types live in @overdeck/contracts (packages/contracts) so server (sequence-io, route) and frontend (DAG) share one schema. parseSequence/writeSequence/reconcileSequence/planBatches live in src/lib/backlog/sequence-io.ts.

Cost/acceptance (operator-noted): the work agent verifies the Sequencer on fixtures + a small representative subset of issues; it does NOT run a full Opus creation pass over all 522 live issues during the work phase. The first real creation pass that commits the production sequence.md is operator-triggered post-merge (reversible; operator can trigger anytime).

Enums (PRD §6): size{XS,S,M,L,XL}; importance{critical,high,medium,low}; score 0-100; condition{ok,needs-refinement,stale}; pass{creation,incremental,review}; edge type{unblocks,informs}; edge source{github-ref,operator,ai-inferred} + confidence 0-1; gate{auto,ready,blocked}; planningPolicy{skip,auto,interactive}.

Tiers (operator-confirmed §15): Now/Next/Later/Someday are DERIVED from rank/score (not AI fields). Now = active agent capacity (maxAgents). Render budget: a tier view renders as a ReactFlow+dagre DAG up to ~150 nodes, else a virtualized ranked list; the pinned/in-pipeline set is ALWAYS shown in every tier view; Someday is a list by default.

Only the internal `pan backlog finalize` command ships (the deterministic write path). The user-facing `pan backlog sequence` CLI verb + bundled `/pan-backlog` skill (D6 'future' in the PRD) are DEFERRED — the v1 trigger is the page button -> POST /api/backlog/sequence/regenerate.

hazards.summary: The Role union is at src/lib/agents.ts:63 (the PRD misstated config-yaml.ts). Adding 'sequencer' requires FOUR edits or typecheck/Settings break: (1) agents.ts Role union; (2) config-yaml.ts DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS + DEFAULT_ROLES (~L369/L387); (3) settings-api.ts ROLE_NAMES (L279); (4) RolesPanel.tsx ROLES array (L88-149).
hazards.mitigation: Edit all four sites in the role-registration bead; run npm run typecheck after.

hazards.summary: derivePipelineState (src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/issuePipelineState.ts) is FRONTEND-only. The backend route must NOT import it.
hazards.mitigation: Compute 'in-pipeline' server-side from ReviewStatus (src/lib/review-status.ts) + live agent state + workspace/branch existence.

hazards.summary: No execSync in dashboard-server-reachable code (routes/services) — it freezes the event loop (PAN-70).
hazards.mitigation: Use promisified exec / spawn; defer SQLite reads off the event loop; sleep via await new Promise(setTimeout).

hazards.summary: Any debounce/backoff/delay test (e.g. queueAutoCommit debounce) must use fake timers.
hazards.mitigation: vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(); never real waits, never maxForks:1 masking.

hazards.summary: NFR-5 footprint budget: naive triplicated rationale ~120k tokens at 522 issues — rejected.
hazards.mitigation: Store rationale ONCE: full paragraph only for the active top tier (~top 80), one-line `why` (<=140 chars) for everyone; markdown table is a compact index. Parser/writer bead asserts per-issue footprint stays in budget.

hazards.summary: Do NOT conflate vBRIEF/beads INTRA-issue edges (PlanDAG.tsx, between an issue's own work items) with backlog CROSS-issue dependency edges (between issues). Separate graphs.
hazards.mitigation: Backlog edges live only in sequence.md (dependsOn + edges with source/confidence).

hazards.summary: PAN-1124 single-spec invariant is unaffected: sequence.md is a NEW artifact, not a spec.
hazards.mitigation: Never touch .pan/specs/ immutability; sequence.md rides the same .pan/ auto-commit but is independent.

hazards.summary: GitHub has no native blocks/blocked-by (github.ts:342 getChildIssues returns []). Cross-issue edges come only from issue-body refs (#N / 'blocked by PAN-N'), operator-authored edges, and ai-inferred.
hazards.mitigation: Read-only: parse bodies for github-ref edges; never write deps back to GitHub. Each edge stores source + confidence; authoritative (github-ref/operator) overrides ai-inferred and conflicts are surfaced.

hazards.summary: spawnSequencer must refuse to run inside a workspace devcontainer and must enforce a singleton gate (no concurrent passes corrupting sequence.md).
hazards.mitigation: Mirror isFlywheelDevcontainerRuntime() guard + an active-run gate like resolveLiveFlywheelRunId.

hazards.summary: Because the Flywheel surgery (D8/#9) is deferred, the operator Block gate stays functional via the EXISTING parked-label skip: Block applies a BLOCKER_LABELS label (needs-design default; src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-eligibility.ts:10) which the current Flywheel already honors.
hazards.mitigation: The gate write endpoint applies/clears the parked label; do not depend on the deferred #9 integration for Block to bite.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1866: Backlog Sequencer

Discovery complete. Verified PRD references against codebase (flywheel project-scoped-role precedent; tab/route/DAG/RolesPanel/schema/auto-commit patterns). Operator confirmed scope split: Flywheel-executor surgery (D8/#9) deferred to follow-up; this issue ships Sequencer + page + sequence.md + cache + pinning + gate + conditions + planning-policy-field. vBRIEF written with 19 beads.
